artPrompt: A long carnival midway recedes into smoky darkness, flanked by tattered striped tents and a looming rigged wheel, strings of lurid lights swagging overhead and drifting ember sparks above scattered coins in the mud. No figures, only the seductive sinister fairway under sickly neon. Wide landscape perspective, carnival red and hot gold washes bleeding into deep black. Rendered as loose ink-and-watercolor — confident brush linework, bleeding translucent washes, a limited palette, generous negative space.
name: The Last-Chance Midway
text:
{T}: Add {B} or {R}.
{T}: Add one mana of any color, but only if a die was rolled or a coin flipped at the table this turn. The lights only come on for paying customers.
flavorText: The midway never closes. It just runs out of suckers and moves to the next town.

extras.dice: any-color mana keyed to a roll or flip happening
